channel.title: Py4_ - A lens into the entropy of being
channel.description: Personal blog about software engineering, life, and deep thoughts. Writing from Tehran to Canada.

item.title: I Asked 3 AI Agents to "Make It Better" Until They Refused. Only One Did.
item.description: I tested Claude Opus 4.6, Gemini 3.1 Pro, and Codex GPT-5.4 to see if they'd push back when asked to keep improving already-finished code. The results split three ways.

item.title: The Priesthood of System Design
item.description: People say LLMs can code but can't do system design. I disagree. System design is more pattern-matching than people admit — and if you give an LLM enough context, it can outdesign 99% of engineers.

item.title: I Miss Coding
item.description: Coding agents have transformed my work at Google. But even if I keep my job, I don't enjoy this new role. I used to love figuring out the 'how.' Now the agent does it, and doing it myself is just inefficient.

item.title: Browser Sessions Are All-or-Nothing
item.description: Web sessions assume a human is in control. But when AI agents take over your browser, you're either logged in with full permissions or not. We need selective authorization—read-only modes, proof-of-human-intent, and agent-aware backends.
